title: What is the Monitoring Period process in Trade-in?
bodyHtml:
This process helps you closely monitor your underperforming quality indicators (KPIs), available in the Quality Dashboard, in order to ensure long-term quality and improve the customer experience.
 

For each KPI referenced in the table at the end of the article, we have determined a threshold above which we consider that you are well below the Quality expectations as mentioned in our Charter. We therefore expect you to improve this indicator so that you can continue to buy products on our Platform. This process applies if you have a significant number of cases in the last 30 days or if your indicator remained above threshold over a significant time period of up to 6 months.


When you reach the quality threshold of a KPI, you are informed by email of your entry into the Monitoring Period process for the indicator in question. You have a set number of days to improve against the cutoff while your sales capacity is capped at 150% of your average daily trade volume over the past 30 days. 

 

During this period, your Back Market point of contact is at your disposal to help you set up an action plan to improve the concerned indicator.
 

If you do not meet the goal at the end of the period, you will be given one last chance ("Last Chance") before proceeding to terminate our collaboration and close your account. During this last chance, the same duration as the Monitoring Period process, you will be limited to 30% of your average daily resume volume over the last 30 days.
 

In addition, in the event that your performance continues to deteriorate, below our critical rates defined in the table below, you will enter Last Chance early and your sales capacity will be limited accordingly.

 

Monitoring Period thresholds and duration for each KPI

 
KPIThresholdCritical ThresholdDuration
Counter-offer rate≥33%≥50%28 days
Late processing rate≥15%≥40%28 days
Late response rate≥15%≥40%28 days
Counteroffer acceptance rate ≤60%/28 days



For example, if, on the last 30 days, you have an average Late processing rate above 15% you are eligible for the monitoring period process You will then get a formal notification and have 28 days to improve your quality metric.
